output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Fight Back and Get Your Money Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Know your rights, learn what landlords can legally deduct, and send a demand letter in 60 sec for $19.99.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (And Why They Might Be Wrong)
What a Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
Tenant Rights: How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit?
Step-by-Step: What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Actually Works
Get Your Deposit Back in 60 Seconds: The No-Stress Demand Letter Solution
key_points: Landlords must return security deposits within a state-specific timeframe and provide an itemized deduction list; failure to do so often forfeits their right to keep any of it.
Normal wear and tear, such as faded paint or minor carpet wear, cannot be deducted—only actual damage beyond ordinary use or unpaid rent.
A formal security deposit dispute letter is the most effective first step to demand repayment before escalating to small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate and send a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord hold your money hostage. Generate your deposit return letter of demand at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no sign-up, no subscription, no stress.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who are problem-aware, have had their security deposit withheld or not returned, and are actively searching online for their tenant rights and actionable next steps.
